project.name: komga-reader
project.description: Komga E-Ink Reader — native Android (Kotlin/Compose) reader for Onyx Boox, source/device-agnostic behind two seams.

nodes.name: DownloadInstrumentedTest.kt
nodes.summary: Instrumented E2E test exercising the full download-then-offline-render path: fetches a book from a live Komga server, stores it via DownloadManager, reads it back and renders the first page with MuPDF.
nodes.languageNotes: AndroidJUnit4 instrumented test hitting a real local test-Komga at 10.0.2.2:25600 (emulator host loopback).

nodes.name: EpubReaderInstrumentedTest.kt
nodes.summary: Instrumented E2E test downloading an EPUB from the live test-Komga and verifying MuPDF renders a non-empty first page.

nodes.name: LibraryFlowInstrumentedTest.kt
nodes.summary: Instrumented E2E test for the library-browsing flow against a live Komga server, persisting server config and listing series.

nodes.name: ReaderFlowInstrumentedTest.kt
nodes.summary: Instrumented E2E test for the reader flow, validating read-progress handling against a live Komga server.

nodes.name: SetupServerTest.kt
nodes.summary: One-time setup test that writes the real NAS server configuration into the app Room database using the same DB name, migrations and keystore alias as production DataModule.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the production DB name and migration list rather than an in-memory DB so the persisted config survives for the app.

nodes.name: AndroidManifest.xml
nodes.summary: Android application manifest declaring the Hilt Application class, MainActivity entry point, permissions and app-level configuration.

nodes.name: ColorPipelineTransformation.kt
nodes.summary: Coil image Transformation that applies the full E-Ink pixel pipeline (saturation, contrast, gamma, sharpen, dither) to decoded reader-page bitmaps, with a profile-derived cache key for per-image+profile caching.

nodes.name: KomgaSourceProvider.kt
nodes.summary: Factory that constructs a concrete KomgaSource from a persisted ServerConfig, returning null when not connected. Bridges the app shell to Seam A (sources).

nodes.name: LocalLibrary.kt
nodes.summary: Extension functions that assemble an offline series list from downloaded books, grouping by series and exposing the set of series IDs that have local downloads for the offline badge.
nodes.languageNotes: Kotlin extension functions on List<DownloadedBook> — pure transformations, no side effects.

nodes.name: HardwareButtons.kt
nodes.summary: Singleton HardwareButtonBus that distributes physical button events (volume keys = page turn) to the active reader via a MutableSharedFlow.

nodes.name: NoOpEinkController.kt
nodes.summary: Fallback EinkController for non-Boox devices and the emulator: reports no e-ink capability and no-ops refresh/contrast/invert while still relaying hardware button events from the bus.
nodes.languageNotes: Null-Object pattern implementation of the domain EinkController interface (Seam B).

nodes.name: Localization.kt
nodes.summary: Tiny i18n helper exposing the LocalStrings composition local / language plumbing for the app.

nodes.name: KomgaReaderApp.kt
nodes.summary: Hilt application entry point that initializes DI and logs whether the device is Onyx hardware (active e-ink mode) or a no-op fallback.

nodes.name: MainActivity.kt
nodes.summary: Single-activity entry point hosting the Compose navigation graph (home, series, group, reader routes), wiring hardware volume keys to page turns, fullscreen immersive mode, theme, language, e-ink mode and the active color filter.
nodes.languageNotes: Provides app-wide CompositionLocals (strings, e-ink mode, image filter, color profile) and overrides onKeyDown to convert volume keys into page-turn ButtonEvents.

nodes.name: AnchoredMenuPopup.kt
nodes.summary: Flat E-Ink popup container anchored to an absolute window offset with a custom PopupPositionProvider that clamps inside the window and supports start/end edge alignment.

nodes.name: EinkBottomBar.kt
nodes.summary: Onyx-look floating bottom navigation bar with equal-width items (icon over label), a black accent bar on the active item, flat with no Material indicator pill and no animation.

nodes.name: EinkButtons.kt
nodes.summary: EinkOutlinedButton wrapper giving every outlined button a consistent 1.5dp hairline border for legibility on e-ink, replacing the too-thin Material default.

nodes.name: EinkComponents.kt
nodes.summary: Reusable Onyx-look E-Ink building blocks: settings tiles, section headers, checkmark choice rows, ± stepper rows and a sub-page scaffold — all flat, hairline-bordered, animation-free.

nodes.name: EinkSearchBar.kt
nodes.summary: Slim E-Ink search field: a low rounded pill with hairline border built on BasicTextField, placeholder, optional clear button and a search icon, submitting on IME-Search.

nodes.name: FilteredImage.kt
nodes.summary: App-wide color-filter image wrappers: FilteredAsyncImage (Coil) and FilteredImage (MuPDF bitmap) apply the active ColorProfile's filter, plus the LocalImageFilter composition local and a ColorProfile-to-ColorFilter converter.

nodes.name: onCreate
nodes.summary: Sets up immersive fullscreen and the Compose content: provides app-wide CompositionLocals and builds the NavHost with home, series, group and reader routes.

nodes.name: onKeyDown
nodes.summary: Translates physical volume-up/down key presses into PAGE_PREV/PAGE_NEXT button events on the HardwareButtonBus.

nodes.name: HardwareButtonBus
nodes.summary: Injectable singleton broadcasting hardware button events to the active reader through a buffered MutableSharedFlow.

nodes.name: NoOpEinkController
nodes.summary: No-op EinkController implementation for non-Boox devices, reporting no e-ink hardware and relaying button events from the bus.

nodes.name: ColorPipelineTransformation
nodes.summary: Coil Transformation applying the E-Ink pixel pipeline to a decoded bitmap with a profile-derived cache key, used only on reader-page requests.

nodes.name: SettingsTile
nodes.summary: Hairline-bordered settings tile composable with leading icon, title, summary and optional drill-in chevron.

nodes.name: ChoiceRow
nodes.summary: Selectable row composable using a trailing checkmark instead of a radio circle, with query highlighting for search matches.

nodes.name: SubPageScaffold
nodes.summary: Scaffold composable for settings sub-pages with a back-arrow top bar and a scrolling padded body.

nodes.name: EinkBottomBar
nodes.summary: Floating Onyx-look bottom navigation bar rendering equal-width nav cells with an active accent bar.

nodes.name: EinkSearchBar
nodes.summary: Slim E-Ink search field built on BasicTextField with hairline pill styling, optional clear button and IME-search submission.

nodes.name: AnchoredMenuPopup
nodes.summary: Bordered flat E-Ink popup container positioned at an absolute window anchor.

nodes.name: EinkOutlinedButton
nodes.summary: Outlined button wrapper applying a consistent 1.5dp hairline border for e-ink legibility.

nodes.name: FilteredAsyncImage
nodes.summary: Coil AsyncImage wrapper that applies the active color profile filter, with an override for the profile editor's live preview.

nodes.name: localSeries
nodes.summary: Extension building an offline series list from downloaded books, grouped by series with stored cover/title metadata.

nodes.name: FilteredReaderImage.kt
nodes.summary: Reader image composable that applies the e-ink color pipeline (saturation/contrast/brightness) to decoded page bitmaps before display.
nodes.languageNotes: Jetpack Compose composable wiring the domain PixelPipeline / ColorProfile into image rendering.

nodes.name: HighlightText.kt
nodes.summary: Small text composable that highlights matching substrings, used by the settings search to mark query hits.

nodes.name: LoadingIndicator.kt
nodes.summary: E-ink-aware loading indicator that shows a static text in e-ink mode and a spinner otherwise; hosts the LocalEinkMode composition local.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines LocalEinkMode CompositionLocal — single source of truth for animation gating across the UI.

nodes.name: StatusCluster.kt
nodes.summary: Composable rendering a cluster of status badges/labels for a series or library entry.

nodes.name: TypeFilterMenu.kt
nodes.summary: Dropdown/menu composable for filtering the library by content type, backed by the domain ContentType model.

nodes.name: HomeScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Top-level home scaffold hosting the bottom navigation between library, groups, plugins, and settings, plus the search bar and type filter.

nodes.name: SeriesFilter.kt
nodes.summary: Pure helper filtering a series list by content type and search query for the library view.

nodes.name: PluginsScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Placeholder screen for the future runtime plugin/source loader (Phase 4).

nodes.name: EinkReaderEffect.kt
nodes.summary: Compose side-effect that wires reader lifecycle to the Onyx e-ink refresher (mode setup and teardown).

nodes.name: ReaderEinkHolder.kt
nodes.summary: Small holder providing the Onyx refresher instance to reader composables.

nodes.name: ViewerMode.kt
nodes.summary: Enum of the runtime reader presentation modes (PAGED, WEBTOON, COMIC) selected by the reader host.

nodes.name: SettingsScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Top-level settings screen: a tile-landing of sections with search, navigating into per-section content in the Onyx-native flat layout.

nodes.name: SettingsSearch.kt
nodes.summary: Pure helper functions for settings search: computing case-insensitive match ranges for highlighting and testing whether a section matches a query.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure Kotlin functions, deliberately side-effect free for unit testing.

nodes.name: matchRanges
nodes.summary: Returns all case-insensitive inclusive index ranges where a query occurs in a text, for highlighting search hits.

nodes.name: SettingsSections.kt
nodes.summary: Declarative registry of settings sections with their titles, icons, and searchable terms used by the settings screen tile landing and search.

nodes.name: EinkTokens.kt
nodes.summary: Central E-Ink design tokens (border widths, corner radii, spacing) shared across the flat Onyx-look UI.

nodes.name: Theme.kt
nodes.summary: Defines the monochrome high-contrast Material3 theme (light/dark inverted) with no accent color, enforcing the E-Ink design language.

nodes.name: network_security_config.xml
nodes.summary: Android network security configuration permitting cleartext/self-hosted HTTP traffic so users can connect to local or self-hosted Komga servers.

nodes.name: SeriesFilterTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the library series filtering logic over ContentType and Series domain models.

nodes.name: SettingsSearchTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the pure settings-search helpers, verifying match-range computation and section matching for set and empty queries.

nodes.name: build.gradle.kts
nodes.summary: Root Gradle build script declaring plugin versions/aliases shared across the multi-module Android project.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Project guide documenting the non-negotiable architecture invariants (two seams), module boundaries, and detail-rule references for the Komga E-Ink reader.

nodes.name: build.gradle.kts
nodes.summary: Gradle build script for the data module, configuring Room, Hilt, and the persistence/download dependencies.

nodes.name: ColorProfileSeedTest.kt
nodes.summary: Instrumented test verifying that the AppDatabase seeds the built-in color-filter profiles on fresh install and self-heals an empty profile table.

nodes.name: PersistenceInstrumentedTest.kt
nodes.summary: Instrumented tests exercising the Room-backed server/settings repositories and Keystore credential store against a real AppDatabase.

nodes.name: AndroidManifest.xml
nodes.summary: Minimal Android manifest stub for the data library module.

nodes.name: ColorProfileDao.kt
nodes.summary: Room DAO for the color_profiles table, providing observe/upsert/delete queries for E-Ink color filter profiles.
nodes.languageNotes: Kotlin Room @Dao interface; queries return Flow for reactive observation.

nodes.name: DownloadDao.kt
nodes.summary: Room DAO for the downloads table, exposing queries for locally stored offline books.

nodes.name: ReadProgressDao.kt
nodes.summary: Room DAO for the read_progress table backing the offline-first reading-progress sync queue.

nodes.name: SeriesOverrideDao.kt
nodes.summary: Room DAO for the series_overrides table storing manual per-series content-type assignments across sources.

nodes.name: ServerDao.kt
nodes.summary: Room DAO for the server table holding the configured backend connection and its encrypted credentials.

nodes.name: SettingsDao.kt
nodes.summary: Room DAO for the key-value settings table providing reactive get/set access to app preferences.

nodes.name: ShelfDao.kt
nodes.summary: Room DAO for the shelves table backing user-defined libraries with encoded source lists.

nodes.name: RoomColorProfileRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Room-backed ColorProfileRepository managing E-Ink color profiles, with the active-profile pointer injected from the settings KV table to keep a single responsibility.
nodes.languageNotes: Entity<->domain mapping via private extension functions; built-in profiles are guarded against mutation.

nodes.name: RoomDownloadRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Room-backed DownloadRepository mapping download entities to/from the domain DownloadedBook model.

nodes.name: RoomReadProgressRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Room-backed ReadProgressRepository persisting offline-first per-book reading progress with dirty flags for the sync queue.

nodes.name: RoomSeriesOverrideRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Room-backed SeriesOverrideRepository persisting manual per-series content-type overrides keyed by sourceId and series remote id.

nodes.name: RoomShelfRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Room-backed ShelfRepository managing user libraries, encoding/decoding shelf source lists via the ShelfSourceCodec.

nodes.name: CredentialStore.kt
nodes.summary: Interface abstracting secret storage (API key, password) so callers depend only on the contract, not the concrete encrypted implementation.

nodes.name: EncryptedCredentialStore.kt
nodes.summary: CredentialStore implementation backed by EncryptedSharedPreferences (AES256-GCM master key), with corrupt-keyset recovery and an in-memory-only fallback that never writes plaintext to disk.
nodes.languageNotes: Defensive recovery: deletes a corrupt prefs file and retries once, then falls back to RAM-only storage rather than crashing or leaking plaintext.

nodes.name: KeystoreCredentialStore.kt
nodes.summary: Encrypts/decrypts secrets with an AES/GCM key from the AndroidKeyStore, returning persistable ciphertext+IV blobs that survive app updates; the credential persistence itself is delegated to RoomServerRepository.
nodes.languageNotes: Keystore-anchored AES/GCM key survives adb install -r; CipherBlob holds Base64 ciphertext + IV for Room persistence.

nodes.name: RoomColorProfileRepositoryTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit tests for RoomColorProfileRepository covering profile observation, active-pointer handling, and built-in mutation guards.

nodes.name: RoomShelfRepositoryEncodingTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the shelf source list encoding/decoding round-trip used by RoomShelfRepository.

nodes.name: viewer-type-resolution.md
nodes.summary: Documents the binding 6-stage domain rule for deterministic viewer-type resolution, the Komga reading-direction field mapping (Seam A), and the ViewerMode app mapping.

nodes.name: detail-redesign.html
nodes.summary: Static HTML mockup of the redesigned series-detail screen, used as a visual reference for the E-Ink UI layout.

nodes.name: KeystoreCredentialStore
nodes.summary: AES/GCM encrypt/decrypt service backed by the AndroidKeyStore, producing persistable CipherBlob values and implementing the CredentialStore interface.

nodes.name: EncryptedCredentialStore
nodes.summary: CredentialStore implementation over EncryptedSharedPreferences with corrupt-keyset recovery and a RAM-only fallback that never persists plaintext.

nodes.name: RoomColorProfileRepository
nodes.summary: Room-backed implementation of ColorProfileRepository with an injected active-profile pointer flow and setter.

nodes.name: RoomShelfRepository
nodes.summary: Room-backed ShelfRepository managing user libraries and delegating source-list encoding to ShelfSourceCodec.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-eink-color-filter.md
nodes.summary: 14-task implementation plan for the E-Ink color filter Phase 1: a pure ColorProfile/buildColorMatrix domain core, ColorProfileRepository with Room persistence (v6->v7 migration plus built-in seeds), and an app-layer LocalImageFilter with FilteredAsyncImage/FilteredImage wrappers, StepperRow control, ColorFilterViewModel and settings screen.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-guided-comic-reader.md
nodes.summary: 9-task plan introducing ViewerType.COMIC for panel-by-panel guided reading: pure GuidedNavigator panel sequencing and PanelGeometry hit-test/zoom in the guided-view module, a Coil-based ComicPageLoader, plus ComicReaderViewModel and ComicReaderScreen wiring in the app.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-komga-libraries-viewer-detection.md
nodes.summary: 10-task plan adding real per-book viewer detection (readingDirection through Komga mapping, ResolveViewerType priority rule on seam B) and Komga libraries via a ContainerSource interface, Shelf persistence with v5->v6 migration, and library create/edit modal.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-phase1-2-mupdf-spike.md
nodes.summary: Short Phase 1 plan (2/4) documenting the verified MuPDF engine spike that confirmed the render engine choice, with conclusions and device-bound work deferred to plan 1.4.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-phase1-3-komga-source.md
nodes.summary: Phase 1 plan (3/4) for the :source-komga module: kotlinx.serialization DTOs, Retrofit API, mediaType->BookFormat detection, KomgaMapper, an X-API-Key auth interceptor, and KomgaSource with factory, all verified via MockWebServer integration and E2E tests.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-phase1-4a-app-skeleton.md
nodes.summary: Phase 1 plan (4a) bootstrapping the :app module: Gradle/AGP setup, type-safe DE+EN i18n, E-Ink light/dark theme tokens, library and settings screens with navigation and MainActivity, verified by an emulator smoke test.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-phase1-4b-render-core.md
nodes.summary: Phase 1 plan (4b) for the :render-core module wrapping MuPDF: DocumentFactory.open with a format hint, MupdfDocument and MupdfDocumentFactory, validated by an instrumented emulator E2E rendering all three formats.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-phase1-4c-persistence.md
nodes.summary: Phase 1 plan (4c) building the persistence foundation with Hilt and Room: domain repository interfaces, the :data module with entities/DAO/Database, repository implementations, a Hilt DI module, and an instrumented persistence E2E.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-phase1-4d-library-ui.md
nodes.summary: Phase 1 plan (4d) wiring the Library/Settings UI to KomgaSource: a KomgaSourceProvider, SettingsViewModel with persisted server/theme/language, a LibraryViewModel cover grid, and MainActivity bound to persisted settings, verified against a live Komga with a screenshot.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-phase1-4e-reader.md
nodes.summary: Phase 1 plan (4e) delivering the PagedViewer reader with E-Ink hardware buttons and progress sync: a NoOp EinkController with button routing, series detail/book list navigation, and ReaderViewModel + PagedReaderScreen, verified by an instrumented reader E2E.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-phase1-foundation.md
nodes.summary: Phase 1 plan (1/4) establishing the foundation: Gradle multi-module scaffold with :domain, domain enums and data models (Shelf/Series/Book/ReadProgress), deterministic SourceId, ResolveViewerType, sync conflict rule, and the two seams (MediaSource interfaces, Document render interface, EinkController).

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-phase2a-webtoon.md
nodes.summary: Phase 2a plan adding the webtoon mode and viewer switcher: a ViewerMode in ReaderViewModel, a vertical infinite-scroll WebtoonReaderScreen, and a reader host that selects the viewer with a switch icon, verified by an E2E screenshot.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-phase2b-epub.md
nodes.summary: Phase 2b plan adding the EPUB reader as the third reading mode via MuPDF: KomgaSource.downloadFile to stream whole files, an :app -> :render-core dependency, and format-aware reader rendering, verified by an instrumented EPUB E2E.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-phase2c-download.md
nodes.summary: Phase 2c plan for download/offline support: a DownloadEntity with DAO and DB migration, a domain DownloadRepository, and a DownloadManager that fetches and stores files locally.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-phase2d-opds.md
nodes.summary: Phase 2d plan adding an OPDS source: the :source-opds module, a DOM-based OpdsFeedParser (TDD), OPDS format mapping with OpdsSource and factory, validated against MockWebServer and a full-flow E2E.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-phase2e-guided-view.md
nodes.summary: Phase 2e plan for guided-view panel detection: the :guided-view module and a pure recursive XY-cut PanelDetector (TDD), verified against a realistic multi-panel page.

nodes.name: 2026-06-07-chapter-tile-view.md
nodes.summary: 7-task plan adding a list/tile toggle to the chapter view: a chapterViewMode setting in domain+data, SeriesDetailViewModel exposure, a header rebuild replacing collapse with the toggle, a ChapterTile composable, and a single LazyVerticalGrid driving both layouts.

nodes.name: 2026-06-07-eink-color-filter-phase2.md
nodes.summary: 14-task Phase 2 plan for the color filter pixel pipeline: DitherMode enum, extended ColorProfile, buildGammaLut, an applyPixelPipeline (levels/gamma, unsharp mask, Floyd-Steinberg and ordered dithering), Room v7->v8 migration, and a Coil transformation plus advanced editor UI.

nodes.name: 2026-06-07-lucide-icon-refactor.md
nodes.summary: 8-task plan replacing Material icons with a Lucide-based AppIcons SSOT: a generator project with provenance, a pure SVG->Path converter (TDD), LucideIcons.kt generation, migration of all call sites, and dependency removal with NOTICE update, verified by an E-Ink screenshot.

nodes.name: 2026-06-07-novel-reflow-reader.md
nodes.summary: Multi-phase plan replacing the EPUB viewer with a reflow NOVEL reader powered by crengine-ng: license gate, NDK cross-build spike, ReflowableDocument seam extension, ViewerType.NOVEL resolution, unified ReaderChromeState/ReaderScaffold, CrengineDocument adapter, NovelReaderScreen, typography panel, xpointer progress sync, TOC/search, and font/hyphenation bundling.

nodes.name: 2026-06-07-panel-detector-v2.md
nodes.summary: 3-task plan for Panel Detector v2: a pure recursive BorderLineSplit cutting at frame lines (TDD), integration into PanelDetector with a speech-bubble filter, and 300-page validation, tuning, and a before/after skill update.

nodes.name: 2026-06-07-robust-panel-detector.md
nodes.summary: 9-task plan rebuilding the panel detector robustly: Otsu binarization, edge-seed flood gutter fill, connected-component region labeling, LTR/RTL reading order, a new PanelDetector with a synthetic page generator, PanelGeometry maxAreaFraction/fitScale, a degenerate guard in ComicReaderViewModel, and a letterbox fix in ComicReaderScreen.

nodes.name: 2026-06-07-search-reset-and-type-filter.md
nodes.summary: 8-task plan adding search reset and work-type filtering: a pure filterSeries function (TDD), a shared AnchoredMenuPopup extraction, an EinkSearchBar with chips slot and reset, a non-modal multi-select TypeFilterMenu, and HomeScreen/LibraryScreen wiring.

nodes.name: 2026-06-07-settings-master-detail.md
nodes.summary: 8-task plan restructuring settings into a master-detail layout with search highlight: pure matchRanges/sectionMatches functions, a HighlightText composable, content extraction from six screens, a SettingsSection registry, a master-detail accordion host, and HomeScreen integration.

nodes.name: webtoon-eink-reader.md
nodes.summary: Concise plan for the E-Ink webtoon reader with frame-scroll and seamless multi-chapter chaining, outlining building blocks across the domain, data, source-komga, eink-onyx, and app modules plus verification.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-eink-color-filter-design.md
nodes.summary: Design spec for the central E-Ink color filter seam, covering the source-/device-neutral ColorProfile data model, repository, Room persistence, and editor UI applied to both covers and reader pages.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-guided-comic-reader-design.md
nodes.summary: Design spec for the Guided Comic Reader (ViewerType.COMIC) introducing panel-by-panel zoom behind the Viewer seam, sourcing pixels from Coil bitmaps rather than MuPDF, with E-Ink refresh and fallback handling.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-komga-eink-reader-design.md
nodes.summary: Foundational architecture spec for the whole app: problem/vision, the two seams (sources and render/E-Ink), Gradle module cut, domain model, RefreshScheduler, EinkController, tech stack, AGPL licensing, and the phase roadmap.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-komga-libraries-viewer-detection-design.md
nodes.summary: Design spec for Komga library import and deterministic viewer-type detection driven by ReadingDirection, library content type, and the source container capability, plus the multi-source/multi-library data model.

nodes.name: 2026-06-07-chapter-tile-view-design.md
nodes.summary: Design spec for a globally persisted list-versus-tile-grid chapter view, introducing the chapterViewMode setting, a single LazyVerticalGrid, and a new ChapterTile composable.

nodes.name: 2026-06-07-eink-color-filter-phase2-design.md
nodes.summary: Phase 2 spec extending the color filter with a pure-Kotlin pixel pipeline (levels, gamma, unsharp mask, dithering) applied per reader page, growing ColorProfile with neutral-default fields and Room v7→v8 migration.

nodes.name: 2026-06-07-novel-reflow-reader-design.md
nodes.summary: Design spec for the reflowable Novel reader (ViewerType.NOVEL) built on crengine-ng, covering the license gate, the ReflowableDocument seam extension, ReflowConfig CSS mapping, typography panel, and offline progress sync.

nodes.name: 2026-06-07-panel-detector-v2-line-detection-design.md
nodes.summary: Design spec for panel-detector v2 adding axis-parallel frame-line detection and a speech-bubble filter to the existing guided-view pipeline, with a 300-image acceptance gate.

nodes.name: 2026-06-07-robust-panel-detector-design.md
nodes.summary: Design spec for a robust panel detector using a hybrid flood-fill plus connected-components algorithm with degenerate fallbacks and integration fixes for correct end-to-end guided reading.

nodes.name: 2026-06-07-search-reset-and-type-filter-design.md
nodes.summary: Design spec adding a search clear-button and work-type filter chips while browsing, including a shared AnchoredMenuPopup DRY refactor and HomeScreen/LibraryScreen wiring.

nodes.name: 2026-06-07-settings-master-detail-design.md
nodes.summary: Design spec for a master-detail settings screen driven by a section registry single-source-of-truth, extracted content composables, and live search with result highlighting.

nodes.name: build.gradle.kts
nodes.summary: Gradle build config for the pure-Kotlin domain module, deliberately free of Android, network, and source dependencies to enforce the architecture seams.

nodes.name: ColorFilterMatrix.kt
nodes.summary: Builds a row-major 4x5 ColorMatrix combining saturation (Rec.709 luminance), contrast, and brightness into a single GPU-compatible linear transform for covers and reader pages.
nodes.languageNotes: Analytically composes contrast/brightness scale+offset into one matrix to avoid chaining multiple ColorMatrix passes.

nodes.name: buildColorMatrix
nodes.summary: Computes the 20-element row-major color matrix for given saturation, contrast, and brightness in the 0..255 value range.

nodes.name: PixelPipeline.kt
nodes.summary: Pure-Kotlin Phase-2 pixel kernel processing ARGB arrays in-place through linear filter, levels, gamma, unsharp mask, and dithering, skipping neutral stages for E-Ink reader pages.
nodes.languageNotes: Per-channel bit manipulation on packed ARGB ints; Floyd-Steinberg and ordered Bayer dithering implemented from scratch.

nodes.name: buildGammaLut
nodes.summary: Builds a 256-entry gamma-correction lookup table; returns identity when gamma equals 1.

nodes.name: applyPixelPipeline
nodes.summary: Applies the full filter pipeline (linear, levels, gamma, unsharp, dithering) in-place to an ARGB pixel array, conditionally skipping neutral stages.

nodes.name: buildToneLut
nodes.summary: Builds a combined 256-entry lookup table for black/white-point levels stretching followed by gamma.

nodes.name: EinkController.kt
nodes.summary: Defines the device-side render seam (Seam B): the EinkController interface plus refresh-mode, region, button, and capability value types, abstracting Boox-specific E-Ink behavior from a No-Op fallback.

nodes.name: EinkController
nodes.summary: Interface encapsulating E-Ink device specifics: exposing capabilities and button events, and controlling region refresh, contrast, and inversion.

nodes.name: Book.kt
nodes.summary: Source-neutral domain data class for a book, carrying source/series identifiers, format, page count, download state, and server-reported read position metadata.

nodes.name: BookFormat.kt
nodes.summary: Enum of supported book file formats used by the source-neutral domain model.

nodes.name: ColorProfile.kt
nodes.summary: Source-/device-neutral E-Ink color filter profile holding linear and Phase-2 pixel parameters, with computed properties deciding whether the GPU matrix or full pixel pipeline is needed.

nodes.name: ColorProfile
nodes.summary: Data class for a named color filter profile, exposing isLinearNeutral/needsPixelPipeline/isNeutral derivations and an OFF fallback companion constant.

nodes.name: ContentType.kt
nodes.summary: Enum of content types used to deterministically drive viewer-type resolution.

nodes.name: DisplayMode.kt
nodes.summary: Enum distinguishing E-Ink versus standard display modes that gate animations and refresh behavior.

nodes.name: DitherMode.kt
nodes.summary: Enum of dithering modes (none, Floyd-Steinberg, ordered) consumed by the pixel pipeline.

nodes.name: DownloadState.kt
nodes.summary: Enum representing a book's offline download state for offline-first reading.

nodes.name: ReadingDirection.kt
nodes.summary: Enum of reading directions that feeds deterministic viewer-type resolution.

nodes.name: ReadProgress.kt
nodes.summary: Domain data class for a book's read progress with a dirty flag for offline-first sync and a locator for EPUB/comic positions.

nodes.name: Series.kt
nodes.summary: Source-agnostic domain data class for a series with generic metadata (summary, status, genres, reading direction) and a per-series content-type override for viewer resolution.

nodes.name: Shelf.kt
nodes.summary: Domain model for a shelf (app library) that groups series across sources, carrying an optional default content type used to drive deterministic viewer resolution.

nodes.name: SourceKind.kt

nodes.summary: Compose screen for the guided comic reader (ViewerType.COMIC): renders the full page or zoomed panel and drives panel-by-panel navigation via tap zones and the shared ReaderScaffold.

nodes.name: ComicReaderViewModel.kt
nodes.summary: ViewModel for the guided comic reader: detects/caches page panels, drives guided panel navigation (hit-test, zoom, hardware buttons) and fulfils the shared ReaderChromeState contract.

nodes.name: EpubBytesLoader.kt
nodes.summary: Source-agnostic loader that fetches raw EPUB bytes (local download first, else stream via ActiveSource) and pushes novel reading progress through the same sync path as other readers.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses withContext(Dispatchers.IO) to keep byte/network I/O off the main thread; stays behind the MediaSource seam via ActiveSource.

nodes.name: EpubReaderScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Compose screen rendering MuPDF-paginated local EPUB/CBZ downloads as filtered page bitmaps inside the shared reader scaffold.

nodes.name: NovelReaderScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Compose screen for the crengine reflow novel reader (ViewerType.NOVEL): renders reflowed page bitmaps, wires TOC/search/typography panels, and drives E-Ink refresh.

nodes.name: NovelReaderViewModel.kt
nodes.summary: ViewModel for the novel reflow reader: opens EPUB via the crengine reflow engine, re-layouts on typography changes while preserving anchor position, manages chapters/search, and persists offline-first progress.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses two Mutexes (documentMutex, renderMutex) with a fixed lock order to serialize access to the non-thread-safe native reflow document across multiple IO coroutines.

nodes.name: NovelSearchPanel.kt
nodes.summary: Compose panel for full-text search within the novel reader, showing query input and selectable result hits.

nodes.name: NovelTocPanel.kt
nodes.summary: Compose panel rendering the novel's table of contents as a selectable, depth-indented chapter list.

nodes.name: NovelTypoPanel.kt
nodes.summary: Compose panel exposing novel typography controls (font size, line height, margins, family, alignment, hyphenation) that drive the reflow config.

nodes.name: PagedReaderScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Compose screen for the paged comic reader: a horizontal pager of source page images wrapped in the shared reader scaffold with E-Ink refresh.

nodes.name: ReaderChrome.kt
nodes.summary: Shared reader chrome composables: top overlay bar, status footer, tap-zone hints, and start hint reused across all reader screens.

nodes.name: ReaderChromeState.kt
nodes.summary: Shared chrome/navigation contract (interface) implemented by every reader ViewModel so the common ReaderScaffold works against one abstraction instead of per-reader ad-hoc state.

nodes.name: ReaderContent.kt
nodes.summary: Sealed interface modelling the reader host's content states (Loading, Streamed, Webtoon, Rendered, Novel, Error) used to dispatch between reader screens.

nodes.name: ReaderRoute.kt
nodes.summary: Reader host composable that resolves the ReaderViewModel content and viewer mode to dispatch to the correct reader screen, while activating Onyx E-Ink fast mode for the reader lifetime.

nodes.name: ReaderScaffold.kt
nodes.summary: Shared reader scaffold composable centralizing the common chrome mechanics (black fullscreen background, third tap zones, overlay, footer, start hint) so all readers build on it instead of duplicating.

nodes.name: ReaderViewModel.kt
nodes.summary: Core reader ViewModel that loads a book (local download or stream) into the correct ReaderContent, builds seamless webtoon strips, renders MuPDF pages, and pushes read progress through the source seam.

nodes.name: WebtoonReaderScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Compose screen for the vertical-scroll webtoon reader: a lazy column of seamless cross-chapter page images with E-Ink-gated frame jumps and refresh.

nodes.name: SeriesDetailScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Large Compose screen for the series detail view: hero cover, metadata (summary/status/genres), chapter list/grid, download controls and content-type assignment menu.

nodes.name: SeriesDetailViewModel.kt
nodes.summary: ViewModel for the series detail screen: loads books and rich metadata via the source seam, resolves per-book viewer types, manages downloads with throttled progress, and applies optimistic read/type overrides.
nodes.languageNotes: Composes multiple Flows (baseState + read/type overrides + local progress) so optimistic UI updates avoid full reloads and E-Ink ghosting.

nodes.name: ColorFilterSettingsContent.kt
nodes.summary: Compose settings content for the E-Ink color filter: profile selection, live cover preview, and an editor for saturation/contrast/brightness/gamma/sharpen/dither parameters.

nodes.name: ColorFilterViewModel.kt
nodes.summary: ViewModel backing the color filter settings: observes profiles, manages an in-memory edit draft with clamped parameter mutators, and supplies randomized preview covers via the source seam.

nodes.name: SettingsContent.kt
nodes.summary: Compose content composables for the settings categories: connection, appearance, reader, downloads, language and about, each filtered by a search query.

nodes.name: ComicReaderViewModel
nodes.summary: Hilt ViewModel driving guided panel navigation: detects and caches normalized page panels, hit-tests taps to zoom panels, and steps across pages/panels on hardware buttons.

nodes.name: NovelReaderViewModel
nodes.summary: Hilt ViewModel for the reflow novel reader: opens the EPUB through the crengine engine, re-layouts on typography changes while preserving the anchor, renders cached page bitmaps, and persists offline-first progress.

nodes.name: ReaderViewModel
nodes.summary: Hilt ViewModel that resolves a book into the right ReaderContent (local render vs stream vs webtoon strip), renders MuPDF EPUB pages, and pushes read progress through the SyncingSource seam.

nodes.name: EpubBytesLoader
nodes.summary: Injectable helper that loads raw EPUB bytes (local download or stream) and pushes novel progress fractions through ActiveSource, keeping the novel reader source-agnostic.

nodes.name: SeriesDetailViewModel
nodes.summary: Hilt ViewModel exposing series books with rich metadata, per-book viewer resolution, optimistic read/type overrides, and serial chapter downloads with throttled progress/speed reporting.

nodes.name: ColorFilterViewModel
nodes.summary: Hilt ViewModel for color filter settings: observes profiles, manages an in-memory edit draft with clamped per-parameter mutators, and rotates randomized preview covers.

nodes.name: ReaderChromeState
nodes.summary: Interface defining the shared chrome/navigation contract (chrome visibility, toggleChrome, navigateTo, onPageSettled) implemented by every reader ViewModel.

nodes.name: ReaderRoute
nodes.summary: Composable reader host that dispatches on ReaderContent/viewer mode to the right reader screen and activates/restores Onyx E-Ink fast mode.

nodes.name: ReaderScaffold
nodes.summary: Shared scaffold composable providing the common reader chrome: fullscreen background, third tap zones, overlay, optional footer, tap-zone hints and start hint.

nodes.name: ComicReaderScreen
nodes.summary: Composable guided comic reader screen rendering full page or zoomed panel and wiring panel navigation through the shared scaffold.

nodes.name: NovelReaderScreen
nodes.summary: Composable novel reflow reader screen rendering reflowed page bitmaps and hosting the TOC, search and typography panels.

nodes.name: WebtoonReaderScreen
nodes.summary: Composable vertical-scroll webtoon reader rendering a lazy column of cross-chapter page images with E-Ink-gated frame jumps.

nodes.name: SeriesDetailScreen
nodes.summary: Composable series detail screen rendering the hero cover, metadata, chapter list/grid and download/type controls.

nodes.name: ColorFilterSettingsContent
nodes.summary: Composable color filter settings content with profile list, live preview cover and the parameter editor.

nodes.name: SettingsViewModel.kt
nodes.summary: Hilt-ViewModel für den Einstellungsbildschirm: exponiert Theme/Sprache/Display-Modus/Download-Ordner/Reader-Optionen als StateFlows und speichert Server-Verbindungen quellen-agnostisch über die Repository-Interfaces.
nodes.languageNotes: StateFlow-Exposition via stateIn(SharingStarted.Eagerly); OPDS-URLs werden bewusst nicht Komga-normalisiert.

nodes.name: SettingsViewModel
nodes.summary: ViewModel, das Settings-, Server- und ColorProfile-Repositories bündelt; liefert reaktive Settings-Werte und Mutations-Methoden (setTheme, saveServer, disconnect).

nodes.name: ActiveSourceTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit-Test für den ActiveSource-Resolver, der die aktive Quelle quellen-agnostisch als BrowsableSource über SourceManager/ServerRepository auflöst.

nodes.name: SourceRegistrationTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit-Test der Quellen-Registrierung im SourceManager: prüft deterministische SourceId-Vergabe und korrekte SourceKind-Zuordnung.

nodes.name: SourceCoverFetcherTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit-Test des Coil-Cover-Fetchers, der Cover-Bilder quellen-agnostisch über den SourceManager statt direkter Komga-URLs lädt.

nodes.name: SourcePageFetcherTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit-Test des Coil-Seiten-Fetchers, der Seitenbytes über die Naht (openPage via SourceManager) statt quellen-spezifischer URLs bezieht.

nodes.name: GroupBrowseViewModelTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit-Test des GroupBrowse-ViewModels: prüft das Durchblättern von Regal-Gruppen (Shelf) und das quellen-agnostische Laden von Serien/Büchern.

nodes.name: EpubBytesLoaderTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit-Test des EpubBytesLoaders, der EPUB-Bytes über ActiveSource/openPage und lokale Downloads quellen-agnostisch lädt.

nodes.name: ReaderViewModelTest.kt
nodes.summary: Umfangreicher Unit-Test des ReaderViewModels: prüft Seitenladen, Lesefortschritt und die quellen-agnostische Verdrahtung über ActiveSource/SourceManager und Document-Naht.

nodes.name: NovelProgressDaoTest.kt
nodes.summary: Instrumentierter Room-Test des NovelProgressDao: prüft upsert, get, dirtyEntries und markClean gegen eine echte SQLite-Instanz.

nodes.name: NovelProgressMigrationTest.kt
nodes.summary: Instrumentierter Migrations-Test für die novel_progress-Tabelle (v11→v12): verifiziert nicht-destruktive Schema-Migration der AppDatabase.

nodes.name: AppDatabase.kt
nodes.summary: Room-Datenbankdefinition (Version 13) mit allen Entities/DAOs sowie zwölf Migrationen und einem Seed-Callback für die mitgelieferten E-Ink-Farbfilter-Profile.
nodes.languageNotes: Recreate-Table-Muster bei NOT-NULL-Spalten ohne @ColumnInfo(defaultValue), um destruktiven Wipe durch fallbackToDestructiveMigration zu vermeiden; Selbstheilung via onOpen-Seeding.

nodes.name: AppDatabase
nodes.summary: Abstrakte RoomDatabase, die acht Entities bündelt und je einen DAO-Zugriff (settings, server, download, shelf, seriesOverride, readProgress, colorProfile, novelProgress) bereitstellt.

nodes.name: Entities.kt
nodes.summary: Definiert alle Room-Entities (Settings, Server mit verschlüsselten Credentials, Shelf, SeriesOverride, ReadProgress, NovelProgress, Download, ColorProfile) mit quellen-übergreifendem sourceId-Schema.
nodes.languageNotes: Server-Credentials liegen AES/GCM-verschlüsselt als Base64-Blobs in der Tabelle; quellen-übergreifende PKs via sourceId.

nodes.name: NovelProgressDao.kt
nodes.summary: Room-DAO für den offline-first Roman-Lesefortschritt: upsert mit dirty-Flag, get, dirtyEntries für den Sync-Rückstand und markClean nach erfolgreichem Push.

nodes.name: DataModule.kt
nodes.summary: Hilt-DI-Modul des data-Layers: baut die Room-AppDatabase mit allen Migrationen/Seed-Callback und bindet jede Repository-/Store-Implementierung an ihr Domain-Interface.
nodes.languageNotes: Konkrete Room-Impls werden hinter Domain-Interfaces gebunden (DIP); LocalBookBytes als schmales Lese-Interface ausgegeben.

nodes.name: DownloadManager.kt
nodes.summary: Verwaltet lokale Buch-Downloads: schreibt Bytes in den konfigurierten SAF-Ordner oder den internen Speicher, liest sie zurück und löscht sie; implementiert das schmale LocalBookBytes-Interface.
nodes.languageNotes: Unterstützt sowohl content://-SAF-URIs als auch interne Dateipfade; LocalBookBytes-Interface entkoppelt Konsumenten vom Android-gebundenen Manager (DIP).

nodes.name: DownloadManager
nodes.summary: Singleton, der heruntergeladene Bücher per SAF oder internem Speicher persistiert (store), Bytes liest (readBytes/bytesOf) und Downloads löscht (delete).

nodes.name: RoomNovelProgressRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Room-Implementierung von NovelProgressRepository: mappt zwischen Entity und Domain-Modell und schreibt Fortschritt mit dirty-Flag und gekapptem fraction-Wert.

nodes.name: RoomServerRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Room-Implementierung von ServerRepository: legt Server-Credentials AES/GCM-verschlüsselt über den KeystoreCredentialStore ab und mappt ServerEntity ⟷ ServerConfig inklusive SourceKind.
nodes.languageNotes: Credentials werden via Keystore ver-/entschlüsselt; AndroidKeyStore-Schlüssel überlebt App-Updates.

nodes.name: RoomServerRepository
nodes.summary: Repository, das die Server-Konfiguration als Flow liefert, Credentials beim Speichern verschlüsselt und beim Lesen entschlüsselt.

nodes.name: RoomSettingsRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Room-Implementierung von SettingsRepository: bildet alle Key-Value-Settings (Theme, Sprache, Display-Modus, Roman-Typografie, Farbprofil) als Flows mit sinnvollen Defaults ab.

nodes.name: RoomSettingsRepository
nodes.summary: Repository, das jeden Setting-Key über den SettingsDao als Flow mit Default exponiert und Setter zum Persistieren bereitstellt.

nodes.name: RoomNovelProgressRepositoryTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit-Test des RoomNovelProgressRepository gegen einen gefälschten DAO: prüft Entity↔Domain-Mapping, dirty-Markierung und fraction-Clamping.

nodes.name: RoomSettingsRepositoryTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit-Test des RoomSettingsRepository gegen einen gefälschten DAO: prüft Default-Werte und Persistenz für alle Setting-Keys.

nodes.name: ViewerType.kt
nodes.summary: Domain-Enum der Reader-Modi (z.B. PAGED, WEBTOON, NOVEL/EPUB, COMIC), das die deterministische Viewer-Auflösung steuert.

nodes.name: Document.kt
nodes.summary: Definiert Naht B (Render-Abstraktion): die Android-freien Interfaces Document/DocumentFactory und ReflowableDocument/ReflowableDocumentFactory plus die Wertobjekte RenderedPage, PageSize, Chapter und SearchHit.
nodes.languageNotes: Bewusst Android-frei (rohe IntArray-Pixel statt Bitmap), damit das Domain-Modul rein bleibt; ReflowableDocument kapselt Engine-neutralen Roman-Reflow.

nodes.name: NovelFont.kt
nodes.summary: Engine-neutrale Beschreibung gebündelter Lese-Schriften (NovelFont) plus die NovelFonts-Registry als Single Source of Truth für UI-Auswahl und crengine-Font-Registrierung.
nodes.languageNotes: Kotlin object als Singleton-Registry; family ist der registrierte FreeType-Familienname für den crengine-ng-Abgleich.

nodes.name: NovelFonts
nodes.summary: Registry-Singleton der gebündelten Lese-Schriften mit Default-Familie und Lookup byFamily, das auf den ersten Eintrag zurückfällt.

nodes.name: NovelSettings.kt
nodes.summary: Persistierte Roman-Typografie als reine Primitive mit dem reinen Mapper toReflowConfig auf die engine-neutrale ReflowConfig (Naht B).
nodes.languageNotes: marginFor mappt Presets auf crengine-ng-erlaubte px-Werte (12/25/50), da nicht gelistete Werte still auf 8 zurückgesetzt würden.

nodes.name: NovelSettings
nodes.summary: Data class der persistierten Typografie-Primitive; toReflowConfig mappt sie auf die engine-neutrale ReflowConfig, marginFor löst Preset-Strings in konkrete Margins auf.

nodes.name: ReflowConfig.kt
nodes.summary: Engine-neutrale Typografie-Einstellungen für das Re-Layout reflowbarer Dokumente (ReflowConfig, Margins, TextAlign, Hyphenation) — die Vertragstypen der Reflow-Naht.
nodes.languageNotes: Hyphenation als sealed interface mit Off/Language-Varianten — Kotlin-Idiom für endliche Zustandsmengen.

nodes.name: NovelProgressRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Repository-Interface und NovelProgress-Modell für offline-first Roman-Lesefortschritt: lokaler crengine-Xpointer (anchor) als exakte Wahrheit plus grober fraction für den %-Sync.

nodes.name: ServerRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Repository-Interface und ServerConfig-Modell für die persistierte Server-Verbindung (Komga-REST oder OPDS, gewählt über SourceKind).

nodes.name: SettingsRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Repository-Interface für app-weite Einstellungen (Theme, Sprache, Anzeige-Modus, Farbprofil, Webtoon-Overlap, Roman-Typografie) als UI-neutrale Flows mit Settern.
nodes.languageNotes: kotlinx.coroutines Flow je Einstellung als reaktive Lese-Seite, suspend-Setter als Schreib-Seite.

nodes.name: MediaSource.kt
nodes.summary: Naht A: die quellen-agnostischen Kern-Interfaces (MediaSource, BrowsableSource, SyncingSource, ContainerSource) und Wert-Typen (PageRef, PagedResult, SourceFilter), die jede Backend-Quelle implementiert.
nodes.languageNotes: Capability-Interfaces (Browsable/Syncing/Container) statt eines Monolithen — ISP, damit Quellen nur implementieren, was sie können (Plugin-Vertrag).

nodes.name: PageRefs.kt
nodes.summary: Top-Level-Helfer buildPageRefs, der deterministisch quellen-neutrale PageRefs aus einer Seitenzahl baut; Bytes liefert die Quelle später über openPage.

nodes.name: NovelProgressMapper.kt
nodes.summary: Reiner Mapper zwischen dem geräteunabhängigen Roman-Leseanteil (0.0..1.0) und Komgas seiten-basiertem ReadProgress über ein festes 100er-Prozent-Raster.

nodes.name: NovelProgressMapper
nodes.summary: Bildet den geclampten Leseanteil über toReadProgress auf ein 100er-Prozent-Raster (page == Prozent) ab und liefert über resumeFraction den groben Wiederaufnahme-Anteil.

nodes.name: ResolveViewerType.kt
nodes.summary: Use-Case, der den Lese-Modus (ViewerType) pro Buch nach fester 6-stufiger Prioritätsregel deterministisch bestimmt (Serien-Override → Format → Leserichtung → Bibliotheks-Default → Archiv-Format).
nodes.languageNotes: operator fun invoke macht den Use-Case aufrufbar wie eine Funktion; bewusste Reihenfolge Bibliotheks-Default vor Format-Default.

nodes.name: ResolveViewerType
nodes.summary: Löst über operator invoke den ViewerType pro Buch nach der Prioritätsregel auf und bietet forContentType für den reinen Regal-Default ohne konkretes Buch.

nodes.name: NovelFontsTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit-Tests für die NovelFonts-Registry: Default-Familie, byFamily-Lookup und Fallback auf den ersten Eintrag bei unbekanntem Familiennamen.

nodes.name: NovelSettingsTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit-Tests für NovelSettings.toReflowConfig und marginFor: korrektes Mapping der Primitive auf ReflowConfig inklusive Margin-Presets und Hyphenation/TextAlign.

nodes.name: ReflowConfigTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit-Test für die ReflowConfig-Defaultwerte und die DEFAULT-Konstante.

nodes.name: ReflowableDocumentContractTest.kt
nodes.summary: Vertragstest für das ReflowableDocument-Interface der Reflow-Naht, der die erwartete Methoden-Semantik gegen eine Fake-Implementierung absichert.

nodes.name: BrowsableSourceContractTest.kt
nodes.summary: Vertragstest für das BrowsableSource-Interface (Naht A), der die quellen-agnostische API-Semantik gegen eine Fake-Quelle prüft.

nodes.name: PageRefsTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit-Test für buildPageRefs: deterministische 0-basierte index / 1-basierte pageNumber-Zuordnung aus einer Seitenzahl.

nodes.name: NovelProgressMapperTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit-Tests für NovelProgressMapper: Clamping des Leseanteils, Abbildung auf das Prozent-Raster und 100-%-Completed-Erkennung.

nodes.name: ResolveViewerTypeTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit-Tests für die ResolveViewerType-Prioritätsregel: deckt Serien-Override, EPUB→NOVEL, Webtoon-Leserichtung, Bibliotheks-Default und Archiv-Format-Fallback ab.

nodes.name: build.gradle.kts
nodes.summary: Gradle-Build-Skript des render-crengine-Moduls: Android-Library-Konfiguration, NDK/JNI-Anbindung der crengine-ng-Engine und Abhängigkeit auf das domain-Modul.

nodes.name: CrengineRenderInstrumentedTest.kt
nodes.summary: Instrumentierter Android-Test des crengine-ng-Reflow-Adapters: Open/Render-Pipeline, Re-Layout bei anderer Schriftgröße, TOC/Suche/Anker-Round-Trip und Registrierung aller gebündelten Fonts und Trennmuster.
nodes.languageNotes: AndroidJUnit4-Instrumentationstest mit echten Test-Assets (Fonts, .pattern), da die JNI-Engine ein Gerät/Emulator braucht.

nodes.name: CrengineDocument.kt
nodes.summary: crengine-ng-Implementierung der reflowbaren Render-Naht (Naht B): hält einen nativen LVDocView-Handle, schichtet bei Viewport-/Typografie-Änderung um und rendert Seiten in eine Bitmap.
nodes.languageNotes: Adapter über JNI (CrengineNative); parst 0x1E/0x1F-serialisierte native TOC-/Such-Rückgaben; close gibt den nativen Handle frei.

nodes.name: CrengineDocument
nodes.summary: ReflowableDocument-Adapter über einen nativen LVDocView-Handle: applyLayout/setViewport schichten um, renderPage rendert in eine Bitmap, chapters/search parsen serialisierte native Records, seekToAnchor/seekToProgress positionieren.

nodes.name: CrengineDocumentFactory.kt
nodes.summary: Factory der Reflow-Naht: kapselt den prozessweit genau einmaligen crengine-Font-Manager-Bootstrap (AtomicBoolean) und öffnet CrengineDocuments aus Bytes für eine Viewport-Geometrie.
nodes.languageNotes: AtomicBoolean.compareAndSet sichert den einmaligen nativen Bootstrap thread-sicher (kein Check-then-Act); Assets werden in den Cache entpackt.

nodes.name: CrengineDocumentFactory
nodes.summary: ReflowableDocumentFactory-Impl: open initialisiert über ensureFontManager den nativen Font-Manager genau einmal (Fonts + Trennmuster entpacken und registrieren) und erzeugt ein CrengineDocument.

nodes.name: CrengineNative.kt
nodes.summary: JNI-Fassade über crengine-ng (libcr3bridge): öffnet EPUBs aus dem Speicher, layoutet sie mit Schrift/CSS/Trennung um und liefert die ReflowableDocument-Bausteine (Seitenzahl, TOC, xpointer-Anker, Prozent-Navigation, Volltextsuche).
nodes.languageNotes: Kotlin object mit System.loadLibrary im init-Block; ausschließlich `external fun`-Deklarationen, deren Implementierung in C++ (cr3bridge) liegt. TOC/Suche werden als ASCII-separierte Strings (0x1F/0x1E) über die JNI-Grenze serialisiert.

nodes.name: ReflowCss.kt
nodes.summary: Übersetzt den engine-neutralen ReflowConfig (Naht B) in crengine-ng-Layout: Engine-Properties (echte PROP_*-Konstanten) plus ein User-CSS-Schnipsel für text-align. Reine Funktionen ohne Seiteneffekte, trivial unit-testbar.

nodes.name: ReflowCssTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit-Tests für ReflowCss: prüfen die Abbildung von lineHeight, Margins, Schriftart und Trennung (DE/EN-Muster, @algorithm-Fallback, @none) auf die echten crengine-ng-Property-Keys sowie text-align als User-CSS.

nodes.name: settings.gradle.kts
nodes.summary: Gradle-Settings-Skript, das die Modulstruktur des Multi-Module-Projekts (domain, source-komga, source-opds, render-core, render-crengine, eink-onyx, guided-view, data, app) und die Plugin-/Dependency-Repositories definiert.

nodes.name: KomgaApi.kt
nodes.summary: Retrofit-Interface der Komga-REST-API (v1): Serien/Bibliotheken/Bücher listen, Seiten und Thumbnails streamen, Lesefortschritt per PATCH/DELETE aktualisieren.
nodes.languageNotes: Retrofit-Annotationen (@GET/@PATCH/@DELETE/@Streaming) mit suspend-Funktionen; Streaming-Endpunkte geben ResponseBody zurück, damit Seiten/Dateien nicht komplett in den Speicher geladen werden.

nodes.name: KomgaSource.kt
nodes.summary: Konkrete Komga-Implementierung der Domain-Nähte (BrowsableSource, SyncingSource, ContainerSource): browst Serien, liest Seiten via openPage, lädt Dateien mit Fortschritts-Callback und synct Lesefortschritt — gemappt über KomgaMapper, zusammengebaut von KomgaSourceFactory.
nodes.languageNotes: Hält die Naht-A-Regel: konkrete Komga-Auth (ApiKey/BasicAuth-Interceptor) und Retrofit-Aufbau leben nur in KomgaSourceFactory, die Klasse selbst exponiert reine Domain-Interfaces.

nodes.name: KomgaSourceTest.kt
nodes.summary: Vertragstests der KomgaSource gegen einen MockWebServer: prüfen Browsing-Mapping, Suche, Buch-/Seiten-Mapping, openPage-Bytes, Fortschritts-PATCH/Pull, seriesIdOf, Library-Container und library_id-Filter samt korrekter Request-Pfade und Auth-Header.

nodes.name: OpdsFeedParser.kt
nodes.summary: Parst OPDS-Atom-Feeds (XML) namespace-aware via JVM-DOM zu OpdsEntry-Objekten (id, Titel, Cover-/Acquisition-Links) mit XXE-Härtung gegen fremde Server-Feeds.
nodes.languageNotes: DocumentBuilderFactory wird mit tolerant gesetzten Sicherheits-Features gehärtet, weil Androids Harmony-Parser einige Feature-Namen (z. B. disallow-doctype-decl) nicht kennt und sonst wirft.

nodes.name: OpdsSource.kt
nodes.summary: OPDS-Katalog als BrowsableSource: liest einen Atom-Feed via OkHttp, bildet Einträge auf Series/Book ab und lädt Bücher über den Acquisition-Link (kein seitenweises Streaming, openPage wirft). Zusammengebaut von OpdsSourceFactory.
nodes.languageNotes: Im flachen OPDS-Katalog trägt derselbe Atom-Eintrag Serie und Buch (seriesIdOf == bookId); fehlende Metadaten werden sauber als null/leere Bytes zurückgegeben, statt Komga-Annahmen einzubacken.

nodes.name: KomgaSource
nodes.summary: Komga-Backend hinter den Domain-Nähten: implementiert BrowsableSource (browse/search/books/pages/openPage/cover/download), SyncingSource (push/pullProgress, setRead) und ContainerSource (listContainers) über das KomgaApi-Retrofit-Interface und KomgaMapper.

nodes.name: KomgaSourceFactory
nodes.summary: Factory-Objekt, das eine KomgaSource aus Verbindungsdaten zusammenbaut: wählt Auth (X-API-Key vor HTTP-Basic), konfiguriert OkHttp/Retrofit mit kotlinx.serialization und vergibt eine deterministische SourceId.

nodes.name: OpdsSource
nodes.summary: OPDS-Katalog als BrowsableSource: browst/sucht über einen Atom-Feed, lädt Bücher per Acquisition-Link und Cover per Thumbnail/Image-Link via OkHttp, mit Mapping von OpdsEntry auf Series/Book.

nodes.name: OpdsFeedParser
nodes.summary: Parst einen OPDS-Atom-Feed-XML namespace-aware via JVM-DOM und extrahiert pro entry id, Titel sowie Cover-/Acquisition-Links, mit XXE-Härtung der DocumentBuilderFactory.

nodes.name: toProperties
nodes.summary: Baut die crengine-ng-Engine-Properties aus einem ReflowConfig: Zeilenhöhe als Interline-Space-Prozent, vier Seitenränder, Default-Schriftart und Trennungs-Properties.

nodes.name: applyHyphenation
nodes.summary: Setzt die Trennungs-Properties je nach Hyphenation: aus (@none), oder für eine Sprache das echte TeX-Muster-Wörterbuch (DE/EN) bzw. @algorithm-Fallback inklusive Hauptsprache und Enable-Flag.

nodes.name: downloadFile
nodes.summary: Streamt die gesamte Buchdatei in Chunks und meldet über onProgress fortlaufend (gelesene Bytes, Gesamtbytes); Gesamtbytes ist -1, wenn der Server keine Content-Length liefert.

nodes.name: create
nodes.summary: Erstellt eine KomgaSource: normalisiert die baseUrl, wählt den Auth-Interceptor (API-Key vor Basic-Auth, sonst Exception), baut OkHttp/Retrofit und vergibt eine deterministische SourceId.

nodes.name: downloadFile
nodes.summary: Lädt ein Buch über den Acquisition-Link des passenden Feed-Eintrags am Stück herunter (auf Dispatchers.IO) und meldet onProgress best-effort einmalig am Ende mit (size, size).

nodes.name: coverBytes
nodes.summary: Lädt das Cover (Thumbnail/Image-Link) eines Feed-Eintrags via OkHttp; ohne Cover-Link oder bei Fehler werden leere Bytes zurückgegeben, sodass die UI den Platzhalter zeigt.

nodes.name: parse
nodes.summary: Parst einen OPDS-Feed-XML-String mit gehärteter, namespace-aware DocumentBuilderFactory und gibt die geparsten Atom-Einträge als Liste zurück.

nodes.name: parseEntry
nodes.summary: Extrahiert aus einem Atom-entry-Element id, Titel sowie Cover-Link (Thumbnail vor Image) und Acquisition-Link/-Typ und baut daraus einen OpdsEntry.

layers.name: Domain Core
layers.description: Pure-Kotlin core: domain models, use cases, and the MediaSource/Document/repository interfaces (the two seams). Android-, network- and source-free; everything else depends on it.

layers.name: Source Adapters (Seam A)
layers.description: Concrete MediaSource implementations — source-komga (REST) and source-opds — that map each backend into the source-agnostic domain interfaces, depending only on domain.

layers.name: Render & Device Seam (Seam B)
layers.description: Render and E-Ink device implementations behind the engine/device seam: MuPDF render-core, crengine reflow, Onyx EinkController/Refresher, and pure-Kotlin guided-view panel detection.

layers.name: Data & Persistence
layers.description: Room database implementations, the offline-first sync queue, and the download manager that back the domain repositories.

layers.name: App (Compose UI & Wiring)
layers.description: The imperative shell: Jetpack Compose reader screens and UI, ViewModels, DI wiring, ActiveSource/SourceRegistration and Coil fetchers — the only layer that wires all modules together.

layers.name: Tests
layers.description: Unit, instrumented and contract tests across all modules (src/test and src/androidTest): domain/mapper unit tests, source contract tests, and app E2E/render tests.

layers.name: Project Tooling & Docs
layers.description: Gradle build files and version catalog, the icon/spike/e2e tooling scripts, and project documentation (specs, plans, CLAUDE.md, NOTICE).

tour.title: The Two Seams (Design Spec)
tour.description: Start here to understand the project's whole architecture before any code. This foundational spec states the central design decision: all variability is captured behind two seams — Seam A for sources (which backend the books come from) and Seam B for rendering and the E-Ink device. The companion CLAUDE.md distills the non-negotiable invariants that the rest of the tour will trace through the code: keep the domain pure, keep ViewModels source-agnostic, and never bake a concrete source or device into the core.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: MainActivity is the single Android entry point and the root of the BFS dependency graph. It hosts the entire Compose navigation graph (home, series, group, reader), wires the Boox hardware volume keys to page turns, and sets up fullscreen immersive mode, theme, language, and the active E-Ink color filter. This is the imperative shell that wires everything below it together — the one place allowed to know about concrete implementations.

tour.title: Deterministic Viewer Resolution
tour.description: Before a book can be shown, the app must decide HOW to read it. ResolveViewerType is a pure domain use-case that picks a ViewerType (PAGED, WEBTOON, COMIC, NOVEL) by a fixed 6-step priority rule — per-series override, then format, reading direction, library default, and archive format — never fragile content sniffing. The resulting ViewerType is the deterministic switch that the reader host will dispatch on in the next step.

tour.title: Reader Host & ViewModel
tour.description: ReaderRoute is the reader host composable: it reads the resolved viewer mode and the ReaderViewModel content, then dispatches to the correct reader screen while activating Onyx E-Ink fast mode for the reader's lifetime. ReaderViewModel is the core engine of a reading session — it loads a book (local download or stream), builds seamless webtoon strips, renders pages, and pushes read progress back out through the source seam. Crucially, it depends only on agnostic boundaries, not on any concrete backend.

tour.title: Seam A — The Source Boundary
tour.description: This is the heart of source-agnosticism. MediaSource defines Seam A: the core interfaces (MediaSource, BrowsableSource, SyncingSource, ContainerSource) and value types (PageRef, PagedResult) that every backend implements. SourceManager is the reactive registry mapping a sourceId to its MediaSource, and its getOrStub guarantees a missing backend never breaks the library by substituting a StubSource. ActiveSource is the single thin boundary through which a ViewModel obtains the active source as a BrowsableSource — hiding the concrete provider so no ViewModel ever names Komga.
tour.languageLesson: Kotlin interface segregation (ISP) in action: rather than one fat Source interface, capabilities split into MediaSource, BrowsableSource, SyncingSource, and ContainerSource. A backend implements only what it can do, and a consumer types against the narrowest interface it needs (`as? BrowsableSource`), so adding a new capability never forces existing sources to change.

tour.title: Concrete Sources Behind Seam A
tour.description: Building on Seam A from Step 5, these are the actual backends — and the only place that knows protocol details. KomgaSource implements browsing, page reading via openPage, file download with progress, and progress sync against the Komga REST API. OpdsSource maps an Atom catalog feed onto the same Series/Book model, downloading whole books via the acquisition link (it cannot stream pages, so openPage throws). Swapping or adding a backend means writing a new class here — nothing above the seam changes.

tour.title: Page Bytes Flow Through the Seam
tour.description: This step closes the loop on how an image actually reaches the screen agnostically. Instead of the UI building Komga URLs and auth headers for Coil, SourcePageFetcher is a Coil Fetcher that resolves a SourceImage to raw page bytes by going SourceManager → BrowsableSource.openPage via the pure loadPageBytes core. This is the integration half of Seam A: the reader screens hand Coil a source-neutral key, and the bytes come back through the seam — so the exact same screens work whether the source is Komga, OPDS, or a future plugin.

tour.title: Seam B — Render Abstraction
tour.description: Now the other seam: turning a book's bytes into pixels. Document defines Seam B as Android-free interfaces — Document/DocumentFactory for fixed-layout and ReflowableDocument/ReflowableDocumentFactory for reflowable text — plus value types like RenderedPage and Chapter. MupdfDocument is the fixed-layout implementation, wrapping a native Fitz handle to render cbz/pdf pages into bitmaps. CrengineDocument is the reflow implementation (crengine-ng) for novels, relayouting on viewport or typography changes. A new engine plugs in here without touching the readers.
tour.languageLesson: Both render implementations wrap native handles (MuPDF's Fitz, crengine's LVDocView) through JNI. The Kotlin layer keeps the bitmap/Android types on its side of the seam and exposes only Android-free RenderedPage value objects across the interface — so the pure domain never sees a native pointer or an Android Bitmap.

tour.title: Seam B — The E-Ink Device
tour.description: The device half of Seam B isolates Boox-specific E-Ink behavior. EinkController is the domain interface plus refresh-mode, region, button, and capability value types; OnyxEinkController drives the actual EPD refresh modes (DU fast-mode, GC full-refresh, night mode) through the Onyx SDK and is hardware-gated to real Onyx devices so development never crashes on a phone. OnyxRefresher is a thin Hilt wrapper letting reader composables trigger fast-mode and ghost-clearing full refreshes — wired into the lifecycle by EinkReaderEffect — without ever importing Onyx types.

tour.title: Shared Scaffold & The Four Readers
tour.description: Here the seams meet the UI. To avoid copying chrome and refresh logic across readers, ReaderScaffold centralizes the common mechanics (black fullscreen background, third-tap zones, overlay, footer, start hint) and ReaderChromeState is the shared contract every reader ViewModel implements. On top of that one abstraction sit the four screens dispatched in Step 4: PagedReaderScreen (horizontal comic pager), WebtoonReaderScreen (vertical seamless scroll), ComicReaderScreen (guided panel-by-panel zoom), and NovelReaderScreen (crengine reflow). Each pulls page images through Seam A and drives Seam B refresh.

tour.title: The Agnostic Domain Model & Library
tour.description: Finally, step back to what made every seam above possible: the source- and device-neutral domain model. Series is the highest fan-in type in the whole graph — a generic data class carrying summary, status, genres, reading direction, and the per-series content-type override that fed Step 3's viewer resolution. LibraryScreen renders the series grid with content-type tags and download badges using the same source-agnostic cover loading from Step 7, proving the pattern end to end: one UI, any source, any device, behind the two seams.
